era: 2013-present. Garage Rock. E=7 -- three-piece, Sub Pop, psychedelic and surf textures folded into the garage foundation.

theory_nodes.concept: PNW garage rock continuum: Sonics/Wailers/Kingsmen (degrees 241-244, 1963-1965, Etiquette/Wand Records) → Naked Giants (degree 251, 2013-present, Sub Pop). The tradition skips over the grunge era and returns in the 2010s with contemporary garage aesthetic: deliberate rawness, three-piece format, psychedelic and surf layers incorporated. Sub Pop as institutional successor to Etiquette and Wand.

E=7 streak: The Black Tones (249), Ayron Jones (250), Naked Giants (251) -- three consecutive E=7 degrees. The current Seattle rock generation's energy level. Not engineered by the engine -- reflects actual energy distribution of contemporary Seattle rock.

Three-piece all-singing band: democratic ensemble, three harmonic textures, three-part harmony available. No dedicated frontperson. Each member's contribution is fully exposed. More naked than a larger band -- hence the name's aptness.

cross_references.relationship: Original PNW garage. Naked Giants are the current-generation continuation. 50+ years between them. Same tradition, same region, different institutional context.
The original garage arc's closing entry. 'Louie Louie.' The tradition's peak commercial moment. Naked Giants work in its shadow without imitating it.
Noise rock follows. Self-released. The underground moves further from the Sub Pop/major axis.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7 vs E=3. Garage rock's physical attack against ambient rain's diffusion. The most dramatic energy gap in the 249-252 arc. Hard and soft; focused and dispersed.

milestones: GARAGE ROCK RETURNS TO ENGINE AFTER 247-248 TRANSITION
E=7 STREAK EXTENDS TO THREE DEGREES (249-251)
